userMessage: Suggest the best acquisition settings for capturing a 1MHz square wave with minimal aliasing on my MSO6B

response.text:
Best verified-safe guidance is to disable Autoset’s acquisition control, keep FastFrame off unless you need segmented capture, and I can’t emit a fully applyable flow because the needed sample-rate/mode commands were not verified in the retrieved source set.

ACTIONS_JSON: {"summary":"Source-of-truth verification is incomplete for a fully applyable acquisition-optimization flow on MSO6B; only limited acquisition-related commands were verified, so this response gives a brief safe recommendation instead of applyable JSON.","findings":["Verified: AUTOSet:ACQuisition:ENAble can be turned OFF to prevent Autoset from changing acquisition settings.","Verified: HORizontal:FASTframe:STATE exists and should remain OFF for a continuous 1 MHz square-wave capture unless segmented acquisition is intentionally needed.","Verified: ACQuire:FASTAVerage:LIMit exists, but fast averaging is typically not the right choice for minimizing aliasing on a square wave.","Not enough verified source-of-truth was provided for the key settings that would normally define a best-practice acquisition setup, such as sample rate, record length, and acquisition mode."],"suggestedFixes":["If you want, I can turn this into a concrete TekAutomate flow once the relevant acquisition headers are verified.","For minimal aliasing, the practical target is higher sample rate and sufficient record length; those commands were not verified here, so I am not materializing them."],"actions":[]}

actionsJson.summary: Source-of-truth verification is incomplete for a fully applyable acquisition-optimization flow on MSO6B; only limited acquisition-related commands were verified, so this response gives a brief safe recommendation instead of applyable JSON.
findings: Verified: AUTOSet:ACQuisition:ENAble can be turned OFF to prevent Autoset from changing acquisition settings.
Verified: HORizontal:FASTframe:STATE exists and should remain OFF for a continuous 1 MHz square-wave capture unless segmented acquisition is intentionally needed.
Verified: ACQuire:FASTAVerage:LIMit exists, but fast averaging is typically not the right choice for minimizing aliasing on a square wave.
Not enough verified source-of-truth was provided for the key settings that would normally define a best-practice acquisition setup, such as sample rate, record length, and acquisition mode.
suggestedFixes: If you want, I can turn this into a concrete TekAutomate flow once the relevant acquisition headers are verified.
For minimal aliasing, the practical target is higher sample rate and sufficient record length; those commands were not verified here, so I am not materializing them.
